description: In shell, run the package-local validation gate (the script under its declared shell (#!/usr/bin/env bash vs POSIX sh)) and summarize the diagnostics.
body: In shell, this capability runs the owning package's gate and reports the outcome so the next action is obvious. Use when you need an authoritative pass/fail for a shell package before or after a change. Do not run a root or generic command that bypasses the package's real config, and avoid reporting success without the exit code. Actions: take the package path, run bash -n / sh -n syntax checks before execution then shellcheck warnings (especially SC2086 word-splitting and SC2046) and bats tests or a guarded dry-run with set -euo pipefail, and record the command, exit code, the first line from bash -x trace or shellcheck's SC code, and the recommended next step. Validation signals include the captured command log, the exit code, and the first diagnostics with file and line. Risk boundary: surface, do not auto-fix; escalate when the gate requires network access, credentials, or a toolchain change.
